title: (Improvement) Writes config files into individual setting folder when in portable mode
author: HorseLuke
body_snippet:
When mRemoteNG is in portable mode, app should writes config files (.xml) and backup files (.backup) into individual setting folder, not into root directory where exe file exist.

## Expected Behavior
When mRemoteNG is in portable mode, new version should writes config files (.xml) and backup files (.backup) into individual setting folder.

## Current Behavior
When mRemoteNG is in portable mode, current version writes config files (.xml) and backup files (.backup) into root directory where...

comments.author: HorseLuke
comments.snippet:
One more thing.

Should another config file `user.config` (located in `C:\Users\[username]\AppData\Local\mRemoteNG\mRemoteNG_Url_[id]\[version]`) writes into such new individual setting folder when in portable mode? But it will break per-user setting behavior, and change this behavior seems not easy. I'm not familier with .net

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: in new version configuration bits are kept in same folder where mR is installed and perhaps will turn in to fully portable mode, so no files are creating outside of installation folder.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `49b83278` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** (Improvement) Writes config files into individual setting fo

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Ou...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Portable mode should redirect all config/settings file paths to the installation folder instead of user-specific AppData folders, which is the standard expectation for portable apps.
Approach: Detect portable mode flag and redirect all config/settings path resolution to the application's base directory instead of per-user AppData/roaming folders.
